---
title: "Open Source and the FDA"
author: "Brian Repko"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: c/t
    footer: "OSN 2026"
editor: source
---

## Introduction
<!--
- caveat on drugs/biologics vs devices (vs MN in general)
- caveat on R vs python
-->

# Getting to know the FDA

## History
<!--
- timeline with major legislation
-->

## Organization / Submissions
* Sub-organizations
  * CDER --> drugs
  * CBER --> biologics
  * CDRH --> devices
* Submission Types
  * IND (investigational new drug / biologic)
  * IDE (investigational device exemption)
  * NDA (new drug application)
  * ANDA (generic - or new indication?)
  * BLA (therapeutic biologic application) - not sure about bio-similars
  * Device submissions based on risk - Class I/II/III
  * 510(k) - substantially equivalent or PMA - de novo

## History of Submissions

## The ICH

# Code and Data

## SAS (closed) to Open Source in Pharma
